Caption:
The Baseline Era

Prior to the pandemic, congressional trading is steady. The STOCK Act exists, but public scrutiny remains relatively low compared to what's coming.

The COVID-19 Briefings

Late-January private congressional briefings on a novel coronavirus spark massive portfolio shifts before the historic March stock market crash.

The CARES Act Era

Trillions in federal stimulus flood the economy. Trading volumes skyrocket as the market begins its rapid, artificial recovery.

Election Scrutiny

Trading scrutiny intensifies ahead of the highly contested November 2020 elections. Many heavy traders temporarily level off.

The Tech Boom

As the market rebounds, legislators and their spouses invest heavily in major tech companies, riding a historic bull run.

The CHIPS Act

Major congressional debates around semiconductor subsidies lead to highly controversial trades in companies like NVIDIA and Intel.

Inflation & Rate Hikes

The Federal Reserve aggressively raises interest rates. Markets cool down, but cumulative trading volumes continue their upward trajectory.

The AI Surge

The release of ChatGPT sparks massive interest in Artificial Intelligence. Tech-heavy congressional portfolios see another massive wave of activity.

Renewed Ban Proposals

Public pressure mounts as prominent legislators' portfolios massively outperform the S&P 500, leading to renewed bipartisan proposals to ban congressional stock trading.

The Present Day

Tracking continues into the present. Cumulative trading volumes for top traders have reached unprecedented levels since tracking began in 2018.
